key_insight: AdsMind closes the loop between LLM proposal and MLFF relaxation, using physics feedback to self-correct adsorption-configuration search on catalyst surfaces.
engineering_relevance: The framework cuts expensive search effort while improving reliability, making autonomous catalyst-screening workflows more practical for DFT-driven materials discovery.
method: Multi-agent LLM system generates candidate adsorption configurations.
MLFF relaxation feedback is used to detect and correct bad initial guesses.
Closed-loop search repeats until a stable, lower-energy configuration is found.
evidence_signal: Across four LLM backends, AdsMind achieved 100% success on AA20 and 98.8% on OCD-GMAE62, with about 4.11 and 4.67 MLFF relaxations per case and better DFT sign agreement than open-loop outputs on six AA20 systems.
watch_item: Performance still depends on the quality of the MLFF relaxation model and may need careful validation when transferred to new surface-adsorbate chemistries.
paper_title: AdsMind: A Physics-Grounded Multi-Agent System for Self-Correcting Discovery of Adsorption Configurations on Heterogeneous Catalyst Surfaces
